title: caltha palustris var. vulgaris
intro: Caltha palustris var. vulgaris, also known as the marsh marigold, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the buttercup family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various skin conditions and wounds, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in herbal preparations to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and sore throats, promoting overall health and well-being. Herbal preparations of marsh marigold, such as infusions and tinctures, have been used to treat skin issues, respiratory problems, and digestive issues, highlighting its potential as a natural remedy.
